enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural
Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
enriched_requirements.object: upon issuance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Regional Rural Banks (RRBs)
(hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RRBs' and individually as a
'bank' or 'RRB).
enriched_requirements.object: Rural Banks (RRBs) (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RRBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'RRB)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In this chapter, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall bear
the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Credit risk' shall mean the potential that a bank's borrower or counterparty may
fail to meet its obligations in accordance with agreed terms.
enriched_requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Credit risk' shall mean the potential that a bank's borrower or counterparty may fail to meet its oblig

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Deferred Tax Assets (DTA)' and 'Deferred Tax Liabilities (DTL)' shall have the
same meaning as assigned under the applicable Accounting Standards;
enriched_requirements.object: as assigned under the applicable Accounting Standards

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Derivative' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in section 45U(a) of
the RBI Act, 1934;
enriched_requirements.object: as assigned to it in section 45U(a) of the RBI Act, 1934
as defined under Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 202

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
(5) 'Public financial institution' shall have the same meaning as defined under sub-
section 2(72) of the Companies Act, 2013.
enriched_requirements.object: as defined under sub- section 2(72) of the Companies Act, 2013

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the Regional Rural Banks Act, 1976 and rules
/ regulations made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the Regional Rura

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RRB is required to maintain a minimum Capital to Risk Weighted Assets Ratio
(CRAR) of 9 per cent on an ongoing basis.
enriched_requirements.object: Capital to Risk Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9 per cent on an ongoing basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RRB shall compute CRAR in the
following manner:
Eligible capital funds
CRAR =
Total Risk Weighted Assets (RWAs)
Where, RWAs shall be calculated as specified under paragraph 15 below.
enriched_requirements.object: following manner: Eligible capital funds CRAR = Total Risk Weighted Assets (RWAs) Where, RWAs shall be calculated as specified und

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The capital funds for capital adequacy purpose shall consist of Tier 1 and Tier 2
capital.
enriched_requirements.object: and Tier 2 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The elements of Tier 1 Capital are:
Note - In terms of Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Financial
Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025, a RRB shall
make provisions for Deferred Tax Liabilities (DTL) on the Special Reserve
created under section 36(1)(viii) of Income Tax Act, 1961.
enriched_requirements.object: Tax Liabilities (DTL) on the Special Reserve created under section 36(1)(viii) of Income Tax Act, 1961

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such reserves
are part of free reserves and may be included in Tier 1 Capital.
enriched_requirements.object: 1 Capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Revaluation Reserves, which do not qualify as Tier 1 Capital,
shall also not qualify as Tier 2 Capital.
enriched_requirements.object: Tier 2 Capital
Reserves in Tier 1 Capital or Tier 2 Capital at its discretion
per cent of RWAs after the regulatory adjustment / deduction as per paragraph 11 below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Of the minimum Tier 1 Capital of 7 per cent, the PDIs shall be limited to 1.5 per
cent of the RWAs;

and
(3) Any additional amount raised through PDIs over and above the 1.5 per cent of
the RWAs shall also be reckoned as Tier 1 Capital.
enriched_requirements.object: Tier 1 Capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that an RRB complies with the minimum Tier 1 Capital of 7 per cent of
RWAs before reckoning such additional amounts.
enriched_requirements.object: the minimum Tier 1 Capital of 7 per cent of RWAs before reckoning such additional amounts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The following items shall be fully deducted from Tier 1 Capital:
Note: An RRB, implementing the RRB (Employee) Pension Scheme
with effect from November 1, 1993, and amortising expenditure on
account of revision in pension over the period not exceeding five years
(beginning from financial year ending March 31, 2025), shall not
reduce pension related unamortised expenditure from Tier 1 Capital.
enriched_requirements.object: expenditure from Tier 1 Capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note - An RRB shall also refer to Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural
Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025 in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025 in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Defined Benefit Pension Fund liabilities, as included on the Balance Sheet,
shall be fully recognised in the calculation of Tier 1 Capital (i.e., Tier 1
Capital cannot be increased through derecognising these liabilities).
enriched_requirements.object: the calculation of Tier 1 Capital (i
calculation of Tier 1 Capital

conditions: if identified in the course of supervisory inspection
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) The following items, if identified in the course of supervisory inspection, or
otherwise, shall also be deducted from Tier 1 Capital:
enriched_requirements.object: Tier 1 Capital:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The elements of Tier 2 Capital are as under:
(1) General provisions and loss reserves
General provisions and loss reserves shall be admitted as Tier 2 Capital up to a
maximum of 1.25 per cent of the total RWAs.
enriched_requirements.object: 2 Capital up to a maximum of 1

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that an RRB has taken adequate care to ensure that sufficient
provisions have been made to meet all known losses and foreseeable potential
losses before considering any amount of general provision as part of Tier 2
Capital;
enriched_requirements.object: adequate care to ensure that sufficient provisions have been made to meet all known losses and foreseeable potential losses before co

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Investment Fluctuation Reserve (IFR)
A RRB may include the entire amount of balance in IFR in Tier 2 Capital.
enriched_requirements.object: of balance in IFR in Tier 2 Capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The total Tier 2 Capital shall be limited to a maximum of 100 percent of total
Tier 1 Capital for the purpose of compliance with the capital adequacy
framework.
enriched_requirements.object: maximum of 100 percent of total Tier 1 Capital for the purpose of compliance with the capital adequacy framework

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Value of all on-balance sheet items and credit equivalent of all off-balance sheet
items [calculated by multiplying the face value of an item with credit conversion
factors (CCF)] shall be multiplied with respective risk weights, representing
degree of credit risk, to produce risk adjusted value of on-balance sheet and off-
balance sheet items.
enriched_requirements.object: risk weights, representing degree of credit risk, to produce risk adjusted value of on-balance sheet and off- balance sh

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate RWAs shall be taken into account for
computing CRAR.
enriched_requirements.object: for computing CRAR

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
2.5
Note - Investment in securities where payment of interest or repayment of principal is guaranteed by State Government and which has become a non-performing investment, shall attract risk weight of 102.5 percent.

5 Investment in other approved securities where payment of interest and repayment of principal is not guaranteed by Central / State Government
22.5
6 Investments in Government guaranteed securities of government undertakings which do not form part of the approved market borrowing program
22.5
Note -
The risk weight applicable to claims on central government exposures shall also apply to the claims on the Reserve Bank, Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC, Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) and Credit Risk Guarantee Fund Trust for Low Income Housing (CRGFTLIH) and individual schemes under National Credit Guarantee Trustee Company Ltd.
enriched_requirements.object: claims on the Reserve Bank, Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC, Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Sma

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The risk weight of zero percent as mentioned above shall be applicable in respect of exposures guaranteed under any existing or future schemes launched by CGTMSE, CRGFTLIH and NCGTC satisfying the conditions mentioned in paragraph 15(5).
enriched_requirements.object: of exposures guaranteed under any existing or future schemes launched by CGTMSE, CRGFTLIH and NCGTC satisfying the conditions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The balance outstanding in excess of guaranteed portion shall attract appropriate risk-weight.

2 Loans guaranteed by State Governments 20
3 State Government guaranteed loan which has become a NPAs 100
4 Loans granted to public sector undertakings of Government of India 100
5 Loans granted to public sector undertakings of State Governments 100
6 Others including PFIs 100
For the purpose of credit exposure, bills purchased / discounted / negotiated under LC (where payment to the beneficiary is not under reserve) shall be treated as an exposure on the LC issuing bank and assigned risk weight as is normally applicable to inter-bank exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure on the LC issuing bank and assigned risk weight as is normally applicable to inter-bank exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Bills negotiated under LCs under reserve, bills purchased / discounted / negotiated without LCs, shall be reckoned as exposure on the borrower constituent.
enriched_requirements.object: on the borrower constituent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Accordingly, the exposure shall attract a risk weight appropriate to the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: appropriate to the borrower

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note - Entire loan amount shall be risk weighted at 100 per cent 100
15 Education loans 100
16 Loans extended against primary / collateral security of shares / debentures 125
17 Advances covered by DICGC / ECGC
Note - The risk weight of 50 per cent shall be limited to the amount guaranteed and not the entire outstanding balance in the accounts.
enriched_requirements.object: 100 per cent 100 15 Education loans 100 16 Loans extended against primary / collateral security of shares / debentures 125 17 Adva

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In other words, the outstanding in excess of the amount guaranteed, shall carry risk weight of 100 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: 100 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
18 Advances for term deposits, life policies, NSCs, IVPs and KVPs where adequate margin is available 0
19 Loans and advances granted by a RRB to its staff 20
20 Takeout finance
Notes: While calculating the aggregate of funded and non-funded exposure of a borrower for the purpose of assignment of risk weight, banks may 'net-off' against the total outstanding exposure of the borrower -
Note - Intangible assets and losses deducted from Tier 1 capital shall be assigned
zero risk weight.
enriched_requirements.object: weight

conditions: in case of investment in securitisation exposures
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in case of investment in securitisation exposures, an RRB
shall keep capital charge equal to the actual exposure and will be subjected to
supervisory scrutiny and suitable action.
enriched_requirements.object: to the actual exposure and will be subjected to supervisory scrutiny and suitable action

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Instruments
(In percentage)
Direct credit substitutes, e.g., general guarantees of indebtedness
(including standby LCs serving as financial guarantees for loans and
securities) and acceptances (including endorsements with the character
of acceptance)
Note - In respect of borrowers having aggregate fund based working
capital limit of ₹150 crore and above from the banking system, the
undrawn portion of cash credit / overdraft limits sanctioned, irrespective
of whether unconditionally cancellable or not, shall attract a credit
conversion factor of 20 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: factor of 20 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note - In these cases, an RRB shall be fully satisfied that the risk
exposure is, in fact, on the other bank.
enriched_requirements.object: the risk exposure is, in fact, on the other bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A RRB shall furnish an annual return to the respective Regional Office of National
Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), indicating capital funds
and risk assets ratio, in the format given in Annex I.
enriched_requirements.object: to the respective Regional Office of National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), indicating capital funds a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The return shall be signed
by two officials who are authorised to sign the statutory returns submitted to the
Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: officials who are authorised to sign the statutory returns submitted to the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The statement shall be furnished as soon as the annual accounts
are finalised.
enriched_requirements.object: as the annual accounts are finalised

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
enriched_requirements.object: affect:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or Directions, for the time
being in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or Directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
<image>
these Directions, the Reserve Bank̥ may, if it considers necessary, issue
necessary clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the
interpretation of any provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank
shall be final and binding.
Netting may be done only for advances collateralised by cash margins or deposits and in respect of assets where provisions for depreciation for bad and doubtful debts have been made.
enriched_requirements.object: advances collateralised by cash margins or deposits and in respect of assets where provisions for depreciation for bad and doubtful d

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Intangible assets for which losses have been deducted
from Tier 1 Capital shall be assigned zero risk weight.
enriched_requirements.object: weight
